content.full_text:
April 3, 1981

Johnathan Redding  
City of Berkeley  
2180 Milvia  
Berkeley, CA 94704  

Dear Mr. Redding:

This letter will confirm advice given to you in our telephone conversation of March 30, 1981. Your question was whether an expenditure by BCA, a recipient committee jointly controlled by four local candidates, is an inkind contribution to the committees individually controlled by each candidate.

Any expenditures made by a committee controlled by a candidate are considered to be expenditures on behalf of the candidate (unless otherwise indicated), and are not reported as in-kind contributions by any other committee(s) which the candidate may control.

In the case of a committee that is jointly controlled by more than one candidate, expenditures are considered to be equally divided among all candidates controlling the committee (unless otherwise indicated), and the pro-rata share of expenditures of behalf of any individual candidate is not reported as in-kind contributions by any other committee(s) which the candidate may control.

I hope this has answered your question completely.

Yours very truly,

Lynn Montgomery  
Political Reform Consultant
